feature: Inventory and dependency graph: availability SLI / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on availability SLI cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for availability SLI: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover availability SLI, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for availability SLI.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for availability SLI.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for SLO and Error Budget availability SLI inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: availability-sli_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for availability SLI cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for availability SLI; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for availability SLI.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for availability SLI, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for availability SLI.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for availability SLI.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing availability SLI data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling availability SLI inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for availability SLI.
acceptance_criteria: Given availability SLI data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: availability SLI connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for multi-region SLO policy: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for multi-region SLO policy security posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for multi-region SLO policy.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for multi-region SLO policy.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for SLO and Error Budget multi-region SLO policy automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: multi-region-slo-policy_security_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for multi-region SLO policy security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for multi-region SLO policy; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for multi-region SLO policy.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for multi-region SLO policy recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for multi-region SLO policy.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for multi-region SLO policy.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing multi-region SLO policy data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling multi-region SLO policy automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for multi-region SLO policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given multi-region SLO policy data exists, when the security automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: multi-region SLO policy connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for SLO breach investigation: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect SLO breach investigation symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect security.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for SLO breach investigation.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for SLO breach investigation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for SLO and Error Budget SLO breach investigation health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: slo-breach-investigation_security_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for SLO breach investigation security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for SLO breach investigation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for SLO breach investigation.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for SLO breach investigation recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for SLO breach investigation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for SLO breach investigation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing SLO breach investigation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling SLO breach investigation health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for SLO breach investigation.
acceptance_criteria: Given SLO breach investigation data exists, when the security health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: SLO breach investigation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for SLO-driven remediation: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize SLO-driven remediation evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for cost issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for SLO-driven remediation.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for SLO-driven remediation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for SLO and Error Budget SLO-driven remediation ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: slo-driven-remediation_cost_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for SLO-driven remediation cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for SLO-driven remediation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for SLO-driven remediation.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for SLO-driven remediation, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for SLO-driven remediation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for SLO-driven remediation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing SLO-driven remediation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling SLO-driven remediation ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for SLO-driven remediation.
acceptance_criteria: Given SLO-driven remediation data exists, when the cost ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: SLO-driven remediation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
